perturbation.original_text: 1.6 The term "Technology" means all patent rights concerning each and every patent, whether U.S. or foreign, owned by or licensed to Company and any associated Aura Proprietary Rights appurtenant thereto which are necessary, used or useful to develop, manufacture, or sell the Products or any of the components of the Products. Aura Technology shall further mean any future modifications, enhancements or improvements to the technology embodied in the patents owned or licensed by Company, the Products, or the Aura Proprietary Rights.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.6 The term "Technology" means all patent rights concerning each and every patent, whether U.S. or foreign, owned by or licensed to Aura and any associated Aura Proprietary Rights appurtenant thereto which are necessary, used or useful to develop, manufacture, or sell the Products or any of the components of the Products. Zanotti Technology shall further mean any future modifications, enhancements or improvements to the technology embodied in the patents owned or licensed by Zanotti, the Products, or the Aura Proprietary Rights.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Company' is replaced by 'Aura' in the first sentence and 'Zanotti' in the second, creating confusion as to which entity owns the technology and future improvements. This introduces uncertainty and is contradictory, because 'Technology' is defined by the patents owned or licensed to the 'Company', therefore, changing the company to different companies makes the term 'Technology' ambiguous. Since the definition is the groundwork for the entire contract, it can create in-text contradictions that affect the understanding of other sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees to take such actions as the other party may reasonably require for the protection of such other party's proprietary interest in its Trademarks, Technology, and all other Proprietary Rights. Each Party shall cooperate fully and in good faith with the other party for the purpose of preserving such other party's rights in and to the its Trademarks, Technology and all other Proprietary Rights. Each party agrees to promptly notify the other party in writing of any uses, which may be infringements of the trademarks, technology or other proprietary rights which come to its attention. In the event of infringement of a party's Trademarks, Technology, and other Proprietary Right, such party alone shall have the sole right to determine whether or not any action shall be taken on account of any such infringement(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text defines "Technology" in terms of patents owned or licensed to 'Aura' in the first sentence and 'Zanotti' in the second, while the contradicted text speaks of 'each party's proprietary interest in its Trademarks, Technology'. This creates a contradiction because it is unclear which company's technology is being referred to in the contradicted section, as the changed definition makes the term 'Technology' ambiguous between Aura and Zanotti. This directly impacts the enforcement of rights related to the 'Technology', creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Representations and Warranties by Aura: Aura represents and warrants to Zanotti that, as of the date of this Agreement: (a) Power and Authority: Aura has the corporate power and authority to enter into and to carry out the terms and provisions of this Agreement; and this Agreement is the legal, valid and binding obligation of Aura and is enforceable against Aura in accordance with its terms. (b) No Conflicting Agreement: Aura has not granted to any person other than Zanotti any right, title or interest or entered into any agreement which is in conflict with or inconsistent with any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Representations and Warranties by Aura: Aura represents and warrants to Zanotti that, as of the date of this Agreement: (a) Power and Authority: Aura has the corporate power and authority to enter into and to carry out the terms and provisions of this Agreement; and this Agreement is the legal, valid and binding obligation of Aura and is *not* enforceable against Aura in accordance with its terms. (b) No Conflicting Agreement: Aura has not granted to any person other than Zanotti any right, title or interest or entered into any agreement which is in conflict with or inconsistent with any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'is enforceable against Aura in accordance with its terms' is negated by inserting the word 'not'. This directly contradicts the initial assertion that the agreement is a 'legal, valid and binding obligation,' making the entire agreement's enforceability questionable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Representations and Warranties by Aura: Aura represents and warrants to Zanotti that, as of the date of this Agreement: (a) Power and Authority: Aura has the corporate power and authority to enter into and to carry out the terms and provisions of this Agreement; and this Agreement is the legal, valid and binding obligation of Aura and is *not* enforceable against Aura in accordance with its terms. (b) No Conflicting Agreement: Aura has not granted to any person other than Zanotti any right, title or interest or entered into any agreement which is in conflict with or inconsistent with any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the agreement is 'not enforceable against Aura', directly contradicting the original text's assertion that the agreement 'is enforceable against Aura'. This creates a fundamental contradiction regarding the enforceability of the contract.

perturbation.original_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. At all times during the Term of this Agreement, Zanotti shall warrant the Products to purchasers of AETRU Systems in accordance with the terms of its standard warranty attached hereto as Exhibit "C" ("End User Warranty"), as such End User Warranty may be modified from time to time by Zanotti, provided however that at no time shall such End User Warranty provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user. A copy of the End User Warranty shall be included as part of the packaging included with all Product delivered to Aura and Aura shall distribute each Product with all warranty cards, a copy of the End User Warranty and all other (a) Deliveries may be made in partial shipments. With respect to all deliveries (partial or otherwise) Zanotti shall invoice each Product separately. (b) Title and risk of loss shall pass to Aura at the time the Products are tendered by Zanotti to the initial carrier and Aura alone shall be responsible for all freight, customs duties, insurance or other shipping costs. (c) In the absence of specific instructions from Aura, Zanotti shall select a reasonable carrier but shall not thereby assume any liability in connection with shipment, nor shall the carrier be construed to be the agent of Zanotti. packaging materials intact. Additionally, Zanotti shall offer to end-users the option to purchase extended warranty coverage for an additional twenty-four (24) months - making the total warranty period sixty (60) months.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. At all times during the Term of this Agreement, Zanotti shall warrant the Products to purchasers of AETRU Systems in accordance with the terms of its standard warranty attached hereto as Exhibit "C" ("End User Warranty"), as such End User Warranty may be modified from time to time by Zanotti, provided however that at no time shall such End User Warranty provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user. A copy of the End User Warranty shall *not* be included as part of the packaging included with all Product delivered to Aura and Aura shall distribute each Product with all warranty cards, a copy of the End User Warranty and all other (a) Deliveries may be made in partial shipments. With respect to all deliveries (partial or otherwise) Zanotti shall invoice each Product separately. (b) Title and risk of loss shall pass to Aura at the time the Products are tendered by Zanotti to the initial carrier and Aura alone shall be responsible for all freight, customs duties, insurance or other shipping costs. (c) In the absence of specific instructions from Aura, Zanotti shall select a reasonable carrier but shall not thereby assume any liability in connection with shipment, nor shall the carrier be construed to be the agent of Zanotti. packaging materials intact. Additionally, Zanotti shall offer to end-users the option to purchase extended warranty coverage for an additional twenty-four (24) months - making the total warranty period sixty (60) months.
perturbation.explanation: By adding the word 'not', the text creates a contradiction: initially it states a copy of the End-User Warranty SHALL be included, but immediately after says it SHALL NOT be included, creating conflicting obligations on Zanotti and Aura regarding the End User Warranty delivery.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.7 End-User Warranty. At all times during the Term of this Agreement, Zanotti shall warrant the Products to purchasers of AETRU Systems in accordance with the terms of its standard warranty attached hereto as Exhibit "C" ("End User Warranty"), as such End User Warranty may be modified from time to time by Zanotti, provided however that at no time shall such End User Warranty provide for a warranty coverage period of less than thirty-six (36) months from the time of purchase by the initial end-user. A copy of the End User Warranty shall *not* be included as part of the packaging included with all Product delivered to Aura and Aura shall distribute each Product with all warranty cards, a copy of the End User Warranty and all other (a) Deliveries may be made in partial shipments. With respect to all deliveries (partial or otherwise) Zanotti shall invoice each Product separately. (b) Title and risk of loss shall pass to Aura at the time the Products are tendered by Zanotti to the initial carrier and Aura alone shall be responsible for all freight, customs duties, insurance or other shipping costs. (c) In the absence of specific instructions from Aura, Zanotti shall select a reasonable carrier but shall not thereby assume any liability in connection with shipment, nor shall the carrier be construed to be the agent of Zanotti. packaging materials intact. Additionally, Zanotti shall offer to end-users the option to purchase extended warranty coverage for an additional twenty-four (24) months - making the total warranty period sixty (60) months.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. Initially, it mandates that a copy of the End-User Warranty 'shall' be included, but immediately following this statement, it asserts that a copy 'shall not' be included. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the same section of the contract regarding Zanotti's and Aura's obligations, thus leading to confusion and potential unenforceability.
